title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public, inspect local implementation scripts/local/integrations/note-draft-upload.js and compare with any references under /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/note-generate. Focus only on likely causes of blank note drafts after POST /api/v1/text...
summary_text: Reviewed image at [docs/drafts/20260401_ai-security-guideline_thumbnail.jpg](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/drafts/20260401_ai-security-guideline_thumbnail.jpg). 1) Critical — Insufficient contrast and weak headline legibility - The frame is heavily biased to very bright values (average luminance ≈229/255), with extreme brightness concentration near white. - This compresses perceived depth and makes thin strokes/secondary text disappear at list-card scale. 2) Critical — Central mass creates weak hierarchy - Current composition appears dense around the center, which blurs the primary visual anchor and forces the eye to “work” before it finds a read path. - Editorially, a thumbnail this wide should have one unmistakable entry point in one-third/edge-biased space, not a mid-canvas tangle. 3) High — Stroke and edge treatment appears under-defined - Thin linework and fine icon/symbol marks look visually unstable when downscaled. - Anti-aliased/stroke-only details in pale fields will be raster-noisy on mobile thumbnails and can look blurry rather than crisp. 4) Medium — Implied brand contrast hierarchy is missing - Foreground/background tonal separation is not strong enough to establish first/second/tertiary layers quickly. - Any text + icon grouping should separate by at least two tonal levels and one accent color tier. 5) Medium — File/packaging mismatch and performance friction - File is a PNG with `.jpg` name and 182KB at 1280×670. If this is distributed as a “thumbnail source,” this causes avoidable platform/cache confusion and inconsistent pipeline handling. - It is still technically fine, but operationally risky. 6) Low — Thumbnail readability in platform UIs likely to fail in first 2–3 seconds - At 16:9 crop reductions (especially 320px width), complex mid-detail + low edge contrast typically fails at first-glance scanning. Better composition spec (production-ready) 1) Reframe around one visual thesis - Use a 16:9-safe layout with a dominant left/right split: - Side A (40–50%): strong AI/security motif (single icon+symbol cluster), thick strokes. - Side B (40–50%): headline + 1–2 supporting lines, no extra decorative clutter. 2) Enforce hierarchy with measurable type scales - Primary title: 62–76 px target at 1280 px width; very high weight; single line if possible. - Secondary descriptor: 24–32 px. - Body/support copy: 16–20 px max. - Keep 2–3 text sizes max, with clear contrast gap between tiers. 3) Fix stroke quality for downscale robustness - Minimum key strokes: 3–5 px equivalent at source, not hairline. - Convert glow/shadows into solid dark outlines where needed. - Avoid thin parallel line patterns; prefer simplified geometric blocks and fewer path segments. 4) Improve contrast architecture - Set background in cool deep neutral/blue gradient band; reserve the lightest values for small highlights only. - Reserve at least one accent color with large area contrast (not tiny accents). 5) Use composition zones and spacing rules - 8–10% outer safe margin. - First focal element near one of top-left/mid-left or top-right depending reading direction. - Leave 30–40% breathing room for the eye to lock and scan. 6) Thumbnail performance hardening - Export as properly named WebP or JPG/PNG source depending platform: ideally `..._thumbnail.png|jpg` matching actual codec. - Keep file ≤200 KB for web list surfaces. - Test at 320 px width and 160 px width; if text fails at either size, reduce text density and remove secondary symbols.
